title: Tenant-level defaults table — master_record_tenants (subscription-tier) in vector_artefacts + seed-trigger repair + URL hygiene
status_note: Drafted + shipped end-to-end 2026-05-15 (unsupervised run). All 7 stories landed: DB migration 068 applied to dev (33 subscriptions backfilled), broken seed trigger dropped (mmff_vector migration 200), Go tenantmasterrecord package wired into main.go, frontend tenantSettingsApi.ts + /vector-admin/tenant-settings page created, /workspace-admin/workspace-details rename done, docs updated. Runtime smoke incomplete: backend respawn failed mid-run (SIGKILL → air bind race → port held briefly → air gave up after one retry). Code on disk is correct and builds cleanly; needs a fresh restart to take effect. ACs 5/6/7/10/11 (runtime smoke) deferred until backend is back up. Drafted 2026-05-15 from MY_HANDOVER.md cleanup register Stories 2 + 5. Story 2 substrate: introduce a real tenant-defaults table keyed by subscription_id (the 'London HQ' defaults that workspaces inherit from). Story 5 substrate: rename the existing /vector-admin/tenant-details page to /workspace-admin/workspace-details so the new tenant page can take the now-correctly-named tenant URL. Inheritance read-path (cleanup register Story 3) is the next plan after this one — explicitly deferred. Pre-existing bug discovered during scoping: fn_master_record_tenant_seed_for_subscription trigger on subscriptions table references the legacy mmff_vector.master_record_tenant table which no longer exists (any INSERT INTO subscriptions currently fails). Story 00568 repairs this by dropping the trigger and moving seed responsibility to the Go subscription-create path. RUNTIME SMOKE 2026-05-15: AC5/AC6/AC7 verified end-to-end via curl with the live backend. Gap discovered: the route had no RequirePageAccess middleware despite the AC7 contract; added the gate + migration 201 seeded grp_global grant for va-tenant-settings. AC8 (subscription-create seeds tenant row) remains done-partial — no production subscription-create path exists. ACs 10/11 (browser smoke) still need a human in the browser.
scope:
Add a real tenant-level defaults table to vector_artefacts, sibling to the workspace-level settings sidecar that PLA-0032 produced. PK = subscription_id — one row per paying customer / legal entity. Columns mirror master_record_workspaces's locale/calendar/owner fields (timezone, date_format, workdays, region, week_start, primary_contact_email, etc.) but at the tenant tier. New gadmin-only page at /vector-admin/tenant-settings renders the editor. The existing page at /vector-admin/tenant-details (which actually edits workspace-level data after PLA-0032's rename) moves to /workspace-admin/workspace-details to free the tenant URL family.

Repairs a pre-existing DB bug: fn_master_record_tenant_seed_for_subscription on the subscriptions table still references the dropped legacy mmff_vector.master_record_tenant singular table. Any INSERT INTO subscriptions currently fails. PLA-0050 drops the trigger and moves seed responsibility to the Go subscription-create path (sole-writer pattern, works across the cross-DB boundary that triggers cannot).

Inheritance read-path (when a master_record_workspaces.* field is NULL, COALESCE to the tenant default) is explicitly deferred to the next plan — this plan only builds the substrate.
value:
Today the only thing called "tenant" in vector_artefacts is actually workspace-keyed (PLA-0032 surfaced this and renamed that table to master_record_workspaces). The real tenant tier — the paying customer / legal entity that owns N workspaces — has no defaults table at all. Workspaces don't inherit anything; every workspace re-enters London/UK defaults independently. This plan creates the substrate for the user's framing: "tenant represents the global position of the organisation, say based in London. When a workspace is created, it should inherit these details by default and allow the workspace creator to override with local structure."

Two additional payoffs land here: (a) the broken seed trigger gets fixed (subscription creation will work end-to-end again — currently broken at the DB layer), and (b) the confusing URL /vector-admin/tenant-details (which after PLA-0032 actually edits workspace data) gets renamed to /workspace-admin/workspace-details, freeing the tenant URL family for the new tenant editor.
implementation_plan: Task 1: vector_artefacts migration 068. CREATE TABLE master_record_tenants with subscription_id PK + 16 fields mirroring master_record_workspaces (drop the owner_user_id — tenants are owned by the subscription stakeholders table). Indexes + check constraints + trigger function. Forward + DOWN migration files.

Task 2: Backfill in same migration 068 — INSERT one row per existing subscription with system defaults (timezone=Europe/London, date_format=DD/MM/YYYY, workdays=mon–fri, region=euw2, week_start=mon, rank_method=manual, build_changeset_tracking=false). ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotence.

Task 3: mmff_vector migration (separate file, vector pool) — DROP TRIGGER trg_subscriptions_seed_master_record + DROP FUNCTION fn_master_record_tenant_seed_for_subscription. Restores INSERT INTO subscriptions to working state.

Task 4: Go — new backend/internal/tenantmasterrecord/ package (handler.go, service.go, sql.go). Mirrors workspacemasterrecord's shape: sole-writer over master_record_tenants on vaPool. Methods: Get(subscriptionID), Patch(subscriptionID, input), SeedForSubscription(subscriptionID).
Task 5: Mount /_site/tenant-settings GET + PATCH in main.go (gadmin-gated). Distinct route from the existing /_site/workspace-settings.

Task 6: Find every Go path that creates a subscription. Add tenantmasterrecord.SeedForSubscription call inside the same control flow. Since the seed lives in vector_artefacts and subscriptions in mmff_vector, this is a two-write operation — use the existing eventual-consistency pattern (write-and-retry, no cross-DB tx).
Task 7: Frontend API helper app/lib/tenantSettingsApi.ts (the name is now free — was renamed to workspaceSettingsApi.ts in PLA-0032/00566). Typed client for /_site/tenant-settings.

Task 8: Frontend new page at app/(user)/vector-admin/tenant-settings/page.tsx — gadmin-only tenant defaults editor. Form shape matches the existing workspace editor; same field-by-field PATCH semantics.

Task 9: Rename existing app/(user)/vector-admin/tenant-details/ → app/(user)/workspace-admin/workspace-details/. Update pages row in mmff_vector.pages (delete va-tenant-details if it still keys the rename target; insert new wa-workspace-details). Move the workspace editor URL into the correct group.

Task 10: Docs — extend docs/c_c_db_routing.md (new table), update MY_HANDOVER.md cleanup register (Story 2 + Story 5 IN FLIGHT → done), open follow-up note for Story 3 (inheritance read path), file PLA-0050 reference in docs/c_plan_index.md.
areas_impacted: SQL (vector_artefacts): new migration 068 creates master_record_tenants + backfill
API: new Go package backend/internal/tenantmasterrecord/ (sole writer) + new /_site/tenant-settings endpoint
API: subscription-create call sites gain a SeedForSubscription invocation
UI: new gadmin-only /vector-admin/tenant-settings page; rename of /vector-admin/tenant-details → /workspace-admin/workspace-details
Docs: c_c_db_routing.md extension + cleanup register update
feature_list: Subscription-create path seeds tenant defaults row
features_extended: Cleanup-register Story 2 shipped: real tenant-defaults table at the subscription tier (the "London HQ" defaults).
Cleanup-register Story 5 shipped: URL hygiene — tenant-details page renamed to workspace-details (it always edited workspace data).
Pre-existing bug fix: dropped fn_master_record_tenant_seed_for_subscription trigger which was breaking every subscription INSERT.

Shipped 2026-05-15. db/vector_artefacts/schema/068 — CREATE TABLE master_record_tenants, 16 columns master_record_tenants_* prefixed, 7 check constraints, trigger function, fdw_subscriptions FDW shadow, backfill from fdw_subscriptions ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (33 rows). db/mmff_vector/schema/200 — DROP TRIGGER trg_subscriptions_seed_master_record + DROP FUNCTION fn_master_record_tenant_seed_for_subscription. Both with DOWN files. Applied to dev DB; schema_migrations backfilled. Subscription INSERT canary passes.

work_item_backlog.title: Go: new tenantmasterrecord package + /_site/tenant-settings endpoint (sole writer)
work_item_backlog.note: FE-API-0028, EST F5, RISK MED. Shipped 2026-05-15. backend/internal/tenantmasterrecord/{handler,service,sql}.go created. Public surface: Get(subscriptionID), Patch(subscriptionID, actorID, input), SeedForSubscription(subscriptionID). /_site/tenant-settings GET + PATCH mounted in main.go with RequireAuth + RequireFreshPassword + LimitByIP + userWriteLimiter middleware chain. Reads/writes vaPool. Wire shape mirrors WorkspaceSettings minus owner_user_id (tenants are owned via subscriptions_stakeholders bridge). go build clean. Runtime smoke deferred — backend currently stale (see status_note + handover).

work_item_backlog.title: Go: wire tenantmasterrecord.SeedForSubscription into subscription-create path
work_item_backlog.note: FE-API-0029, EST F2, RISK MED. Investigation 2026-05-15: NO production Go code path creates subscriptions. Subscriptions are only seeded via migrations + test fixtures. The Service.SeedForSubscription method was shipped in 00569 and is callable; auto-create-on-first-Get (sqlEnsureTenantRow) covers any subscription that lacks a tenant row at read time. Explicit wiring deferred until a real subscription-create production path exists (admin onboarding flow, signup, billing webhook, etc.). Test fixtures intentionally not updated — they don't call Get() on tenantmasterrecord so the missing seed is invisible to them.

acceptance_criteria.proven_by: MY_HANDOVER.md Story 2 + Story 5 marked 'SHIPPED 2026-05-15 (PLA-0050)'; Story 3 marked 'NOW UNBLOCKED by PLA-0050' with the service-layer fallback decision recorded — verified 2026-05-15.

risks.risk: Two-write across DBs (mmff_vector.subscriptions + vector_artefacts.master_record_tenants) — no cross-DB tx in Postgres; failure mid-write leaves orphans
risks.mitigation: Use the existing eventual-consistency pattern: Go path writes subscription first (anchor), then seeds tenant defaults; if the tenant write fails, log + queue retry. SeedForSubscription uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING so retries are idempotent. Same approach the project uses for other cross-DB writes (per docs/c_polymorphic_writes.md).

risks.risk: Backfill might miss some subscriptions if migration runs concurrently with new subscription creates
risks.mitigation: Migration 068 runs in a single transaction; the broken trigger drop runs in a separate mmff_vector migration. Backfill uses INSERT ... SELECT FROM subscriptions ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING — safe against races. Anyway, dev DB has 1 subscription and the seed trigger has been broken (no new subscriptions creatable) so race window is effectively zero.

risks.risk: Renaming the existing tenant-details page route may break bookmarks / external links
risks.mitigation: Dev-only environment; no external bookmarks. Add a Next.js redirect from /vector-admin/tenant-details → /workspace-admin/workspace-details as a courtesy. Document in MY_HANDOVER.md.

risks.risk: Story 3 (inheritance read-path) is the natural follow-up but is OUT of scope here — risk that we leave a half-built feature where the tenant table exists but workspaces don't actually inherit
risks.mitigation: Plan explicitly flags this. The new tenant page is a standalone editor; nothing breaks if Story 3 doesn't ship immediately. Filed in MY_HANDOVER.md cleanup register as Story 3, now unblocked.
